facebook.caption: Not every memorable stop in Phu Quoc is a beach. Dinh Ba Thuy Long Thanh Mau, tucked along Vo Thi Sau street in Khu 1, is a traditional Vietnamese temple dedicated to the Holy Mother of the Water Dragon, a sea deity deeply revered by local fishing communities. The architecture is colorful and richly detailed, incense fills the air, and the atmosphere is genuinely peaceful. Rated 4.5 out of 5 across 275 reviews, it is clearly a place that leaves an impression. Visiting early in the morning gives you the calmest, most atmospheric experience. Practical tip: dress modestly before entering, covering shoulders and knees out of respect for worshippers.

instagram.caption: Beyond the beaches, Phu Quoc has soul. Dinh Ba Thuy Long Thanh Mau is a beautifully ornate temple honoring a sea deity central to local fishing heritage. Rated 4.5/5 by 275 visitors, it is calm, colorful, and completely free. Go early, dress respectfully, and take it all in.

tiktok.caption: The most peaceful 20 minutes in Phu Quoc, and it has nothing to do with the beach. #PhuQuoc #VietnamTravel #CulturalTravel #HiddenGem

scenes: A temple steps from the street
